title: Introduction to Node.js
subtitle: Test your knowledge on Node.js fundamentals, architecture, core modules, advantages, and server implementation.

questions.t: Who developed Node.js and in what year was it released?
Which of the following best describes the architecture of Node.js?
Which built-in Node.js module allows you to create an HTTP server, make HTTP requests, and handle HTTP responses?
Which core module is used to deal with file input/output (I/O) operations in Node.js?
How does Node.js overcome its single-threaded limitation to support multi-threading?
choices: Using the cluster module to create child processes
By default, Node.js is multi-threaded and does not have this limitation
questions.a: Using the cluster module to create child processes

questions.t: Which method of the http.ServerResponse object is used to set the status code and headers before sending the response body?
What are the three important parts of a Node.js application?
choices: Install packages, write HTML, deploy to server
Import required module, create server, read request and return response
Create database, define routes, render templates
Initialize npm, install Express, create middleware
questions.a: Import required module, create server, read request and return response

questions.t: What directive is used to load (import) a JavaScript module in Node.js?
In the line 'const http = require('http');', what does require('http') do?
choices: Downloads the http module from the internet
Imports the built-in HTTP module in Node.js
Sends an HTTP request to a remote server
questions.a: Imports the built-in HTTP module in Node.js

questions.t: What does the http.createServer() method return?
What is the callback function passed to http.createServer() commonly referred to as?
In the createServer callback '(req, res) => { ... }', what does the 'req' parameter represent?
In the createServer callback '(req, res) => { ... }', what does the 'res' parameter represent?
choices: The result set from a database

questions.t: What does the '=>' symbol in JavaScript represent?
In res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }), what does { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' } specify?
choices: The response headers, indicating the body will be in HTML format
The encoding type for the URL
questions.a: The response headers, indicating the body will be in HTML format

questions.t: What does the res.write() method do in Node.js?
choices: Writes data to the file system
Writes data to the response body in chunks
questions.a: Writes data to the response body in chunks

questions.t: What does Hello.listen(8080, callback) do in a Node.js application?
choices: Opens a browser window at port 8080
Sets up the HTTP server to listen for incoming requests on port 8080
questions.a: Sets up the HTTP server to listen for incoming requests on port 8080

questions.t: What is the package.json file in a Node.js project?
choices: A JavaScript file that runs the server
A metadata file that provides information about the project and its dependencies
A configuration file for the database
A log file that records server errors
questions.a: A metadata file that provides information about the project and its dependencies

questions.t: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of Node.js mentioned in the material?
choices: Better Performance due to non-blocking I/O

questions.t: How does Node.js achieve better performance?
choices: By using multiple threads for each request
By compiling code to machine language before execution
Because its I/O operations are non-blocking
By using a built-in caching layer
questions.a: Because its I/O operations are non-blocking

questions.t: What are the two ways Node.js applications can be scaled?
Why is Node.js described as 'Lightweight and Extensible'?
choices: It can only run small applications
It is based on JavaScript, can run on client and server side, and supports JSON data exchange
It requires very little disk space to install
It uses a compiled language for efficiency
questions.a: It is based on JavaScript, can run on client and server side, and supports JSON data exchange

questions.t: Which of the following is a correct step in setting up a Node.js project?
choices: Run 'npm init' to create a package.json, then run the .js file with 'node filename.js'
Run 'node init' to create a package.json, then run the .js file with 'npm filename.js'
Run 'npm start' to create a package.json, then run the .js file with 'js filename.node'
Run 'node install' to create a package.json, then run the .js file with 'run filename.js'
questions.a: Run 'npm init' to create a package.json, then run the .js file with 'node filename.js'

questions.t: After running a Node.js server file, where do you view the output?
choices: In the text editor where you wrote the code
In the browser at the given port (e.g., http://localhost:8080)
questions.a: In the browser at the given port (e.g., http://localhost:8080)

questions.t: Which of the following is NOT a core module in Node.js?
What is npm in the Node.js ecosystem?
choices: The Node Package Manager, used to manage project dependencies
A browser extension for debugging Node.js
questions.a: The Node Package Manager, used to manage project dependencies

questions.t: Node.js uses JavaScript as a single programming language across both front-end and back-end development.
The command 'npm init' is used to execute a JavaScript file using the Node.js runtime.
In Node.js, res.end() must be called to signal the end of the response and complete the network handshake.
Streams in Node.js are objects that let you read data or write data continuously.
The require() directive is used to load built-in or external modules in Node.js.
The createServer() method takes a callback function that runs whenever the server receives a request.
In Node.js, the res.write() method can only be called once per response.
Node.js is open source and free to use with no license fees required.
Node.js can only be used for back-end development and cannot interact with front-end code.
The package.json file is essential for managing and sharing your Node.js project with others.
The 'cluster' module in Node.js allows the creation of child processes to overcome the single-threaded limitation.
When res.end() is called without any data, it signals the end of the response without any additional content in the response body.
Node.js supports the exchange of data using JSON, which is easily consumed by JavaScript.
The '=>' arrow function syntax in JavaScript can only be used inside Node.js and not in browser JavaScript.
Node.js provides REST API support, making it easy to develop RESTful services.
What command is used to initialize a new Node.js project and create a package.json file?
Which command in the terminal or command prompt is used to execute a JavaScript file named server.js with the Node.js runtime?
Which global object in Node.js specifies the name of the directory that contains the code of the application?
Which built-in Node.js module is used to handle streams that contain only binary data?
What HTTP status code is used in res.writeHead(200, ...) to indicate that the request has been successfully processed?
What method is used to write data to the response body in chunks in Node.js?
What method is used to create an HTTP server instance in Node.js?
Which core module in Node.js is used for resolving domain names to IP addresses?
What is the default port number used in the Hello World example from the material?
